audits.cleanReason: Reviewed the chapter's real negation-pivot candidates and found only structural keeps: a binary diagnostic ("Not random."), one spatial correction ("not the bars"), and two hard-constraint lines about what Lor'themar can or cannot name or reach in time.

clusterFlags: smart-POV analytic narration carries four agency-laundering hits across the trial and aftermath sections

findings.line: No. It depended on whether anyone in the room wanted truth before it arrived.
findings.subject: truth
findings.realAgent: the people in the room hearing and crediting Sylvanas's account
findings.pattern: P5 abstract object with physical-behavior camouflage
findings.reason: The sentence makes abstract truth arrive on its own instead of naming the room's human acceptance or refusal.
findings.suggestedFix: No. It depended on whether anyone in the room wanted the truth before they heard it.

findings.line: Each true answer tightened the case instead.
findings.subject: Each true answer
findings.realAgent: Ashcombe using Uther's concessions to strengthen her case
findings.pattern: P5 abstract evidence carrying scene work
findings.reason: The answer is made to do the prosecutorial work instead of naming Ashcombe's use of it.
findings.suggestedFix: With each true answer, Ashcombe had more to use.

findings.line: The field officer in him took the number and left the fear alone.
findings.subject: The field officer in him
findings.realAgent: Lor'themar processing the timetable and choosing not to speak the fear aloud
findings.reason: An internal role-label is made to perform Lor'themar's reading and restraint for him.
findings.suggestedFix: He took the number and left the fear alone.

findings.line: Lor'themar gave the guard a look that promised future trouble in formal clothing.
findings.subject: look
findings.realAgent: Lor'themar signaling future retaliation through his expression
findings.reason: The promise is laundered onto the look instead of staying with Lor'themar.
findings.suggestedFix: Lor'themar gave the guard a look, promising future trouble in formal clothing.

audits.cleanReason: Counted the capped tic families in this chapter: one corner-of-mouth beat, one emotional mouth-tightening beat plus two physical chain-tightening beats, no overloaded "marked," no gaze-cluster overflow, no weight-metaphor overflow, and no performance-tag overflow.

summary.verdictReason: Pass A still has four mild intent-smear kills. With more than three mild-only kills and no higher-severity findings, the pass fails under the pass rule.
